title: Apple Just Built WSL for the Mac (Container Machines)
summary: Apple Container Machines provides lightweight, persistent Linux environments on macOS, built on Apple's container project and optimized for Apple Silicon. It offers multi-distro support, systemd integration, and home directory sharing for cross-platform development and testing workflows.
writeup: The video covers Apple Container Machines, a feature that runs lightweight, persistent Linux environments on macOS using Apple's container project with optimization for Apple Silicon. It matters because it brings WSL-style Linux integration to the Mac for cross-platform development and testing. Concrete takeaways: multi-distro support, systemd integration, and home directory sharing between the host and the Linux environment.
